summary: Mobile Calendar UX Testing - Critical Bug Found: useIsMobile hook not working, Popover shown instead of Drawer on mobile viewport

ui_bugs.issue: useIsMobile() hook returns false on mobile viewport (375px), causing Popover to render instead of Drawer
ui_bugs.evidence: Browser reports windowWidth=375, isMobileByWidth=true, isMobileByMql=true, but component renders Popover with 'bg-popover' classes instead of Drawer

ui_bugs.issue: Calendar closes after FIRST date selection (should stay open until BOTH dates selected)
ui_bugs.evidence: First click sets from=to (same date), calendar immediately closes, user must reopen to select end date
ui_bugs.root_cause: Since Drawer is not used, Popover's default close-on-click behavior takes over

issues: Minor: Console warning 'Missing Description or aria-describedby for DialogContent'

action_items: FIX CRITICAL: useIsMobile hook in /app/components/booking-date-picker.jsx is not returning true on mobile. The hook initializes to false and never updates. Consider using the shared hook from /app/hooks/use-mobile.jsx or fix the SSR hydration issue.
Verify that when isMobile=true, the Drawer component renders (with vaul-drawer attributes)
Test that Drawer stays open after first date click and shows hint message
Test that Drawer auto-closes after second (different) date is selected
critical_code_review_comments: useIsMobile hook in booking-date-picker.jsx initializes state to false, causing wrong branch on first render. This is a React SSR/hydration issue - the component conditionally renders Drawer vs Popover based on isMobile, but isMobile is false until useEffect runs.

The hook at line 27 `useState(false)` should either: (1) use a loading/skeleton state until mobile detection completes, (2) default to mobile-first approach, or (3) use CSS media queries instead of JS detection
There's a shared useIsMobile hook at /app/hooks/use-mobile.jsx that uses matchMedia - consider using that consistently

success_rate.frontend: 60% - Date selection and price breakdown work, but mobile Drawer UX completely broken

context_for_next_testing_agent: Critical bug: useIsMobile hook not working. When testing mobile drawer behavior, first verify that vaul-drawer attributes appear in DOM. Current state: Popover is rendered even on mobile viewport. The hint message and stay-open behavior cannot be tested until Drawer renders.

button_format_with_nights.test: Button shows '6 Apr — 8 Apr (2 nights)' format
button_format_with_nights.evidence: Screenshot shows correct format after date range selection

cell_size_touch_friendly.test: Calendar cell size is adequate for touch (>40px)

mobile_drawer_opens.test: Mobile should open Drawer from bottom instead of Popover
mobile_drawer_opens.evidence: Popover detected (bg-popover classes), no vaul-drawer attributes found
mobile_drawer_opens.root_cause: useIsMobile() returns false on mobile viewport

drawer_stays_open_after_first_date.test: Calendar should stay open after first date selection
drawer_stays_open_after_first_date.evidence: Calendar closes immediately after first click
drawer_stays_open_after_first_date.root_cause: Popover is used instead of Drawer, Popover closes on selection

hint_message_displayed.test: Hint '✓ Check-in selected. Now select check-out date' should show
hint_message_displayed.evidence: Cannot test - Drawer component not rendered

rca_of_the_issue.issue: Mobile Drawer not rendering, Popover shown instead
rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: useIsMobile hook at line 26-37 of booking-date-picker.jsx initializes state to false. The useEffect updates it to true after hydration, but by then React has already rendered the Popover branch. The conditional `if (isMobile)` at line 176 evaluates to false on initial render.
reproduction_steps: 5. Observe: Popover opens (floating card style) instead of Drawer (bottom sheet)
rca_of_the_issue.fix_suggestion: Use CSS media queries with display:none/block to conditionally render, or implement a loading state until isMobile is determined, or use the shared hook from /app/hooks/use-mobile.jsx with proper handling of undefined initial state
